model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current micro-resnet is likely under-capacity for CIFAR-10 under a fixed 256-step budget. A modest width increase in both convolutional trunk and classifier head is a simple, high-probability way to reduce underfitting and improve validation loss without changing training logic.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps and Adam, the current run is likely still under-optimized. Increasing the base learning rate should improve early progress and reduce validation loss within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"After a strong gain from higher learning rate, the next low-complexity improvement is decoupled weight decay via AdamW, which often improves validation loss versus Adam at the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW regressed versus the previous best, so reverting to Adam restores the stronger optimizer configuration before testing new changes.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine scheduling over the short fixed-step horizon regressed validation loss, so disabling it restores the stronger constant-learning-rate behavior with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model still trains under a tight 256-step budget, so a higher Adam learning rate can improve progress per step; 2e-3 is a reasonable next scalar sweep from the current best.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Given the very short 256-step budget, the model is still likely optimization-limited; slightly reducing weight decay should ease regularization pressure and can improve validation loss at this stage.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Reducing weight decay regressed validation loss, so restoring 1e-4 returns to the stronger regularization setting used by the current best configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step training, increasing batch size exposes more unique examples within the same 256 updates and can improve validation loss with Adam at the current learning rate.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After a major gain from larger batch size, increasing learning rate is a targeted next step to match the larger-batch regime and potentially improve progress within the same 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance has improved monotonically as learning rate increased with batch size 128, so the next efficient search step is another small LR increase to test whether the optimum is higher.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 4e-3 trial regressed from the best result, indicating we passed the local optimum; reverting to 3e-3 restores the stronger setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The largest gain so far came from increasing batch size under fixed-step evaluation; testing 256 is the next direct scaling move to increase examples seen within the same 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size now at 256, the effective gradient noise is lower, so a higher learning rate may better utilize the fixed 256-step budget and further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate at batch size 256 clearly regressed, so reverting to 3e-3 restores the best-performing setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Now that optimization is much stronger, a modest increase in classifier capacity is a low-risk architecture tweak that can improve separability without major convolutional compute changes.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing classifier width hurt validation loss, so reverting to 64 restores the better-performing and simpler architecture.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With optimization now strong at large batch size, the next high-upside test is increasing convolutional width to raise representational capacity; this targets likely residual underfitting in the current micro-resnet.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing model width to 24 channels, a modest learning-rate bump may better optimize the larger network within the same fixed 256-step budget while staying below the previously unstable 4e-3 setting.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}
